description_long: This is a traditional dagger with a slightly curved, single-edged steel blade and a cream-colored handle. The handle appears to be made of bone or ivory, featuring a simple, straight design with a subtle guard. The blade is accompanied by an ornate sheath, decorated with intricate patterns and metallic accents, likely brass or bronze. The sheath fits the blade closely and has a pointed tip, matching the curvature of the blade. The overall craftsmanship suggests a ceremonial or decorative purpose, though the blade is functional. The object is compact and lightweight, suitable for carrying or display.
description: A curved steel dagger with a cream handle and an ornate, patterned sheath. The design is both functional and decorative.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow profile of the blade and sheath, with the handle and tip visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the dagger inside its sheath, highlighting the ornate sheath pattern and handle shape.
description_view_2: Top-down view revealing the width of the blade and sheath, and the alignment of the handle.
description_view_3: Side view with the blade unsheathed, showing the curved blade, handle, and the detailed sheath separately.
attribute_handle_design: straight, cream-colored, likely bone or ivory, simple with a subtle guard
attribute_ornamentation: ornate sheath with intricate patterns and metallic accents
